channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Iran begins funeral rites for Ali Khamenei, supreme leader killed in war
item.dc:creator: Susannah George
item.description: For the surviving Iranian regime, the funeral offers an opportunity to project power after withstanding months of war with Israel and the United States.

item.title: Kyiv’s most-bombed neighborhood just won’t quit
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix, Serhiy Morgunov, Kostiantyn Khudov
item.description: After four years of war, life in Lukianivka, the most frequently hit part of the Ukrainian capital, is a mix of adaptability and endurance, defiance and denial, resignation and resilience.

item.title: Vatican excommunicates bishops of breakaway traditionalist sect
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola, Stefano Pitrelli
item.description: The sect, the Society of St. Pius X, defied a direct plea from Pope Leo XIV and sought to consecrate four bishops without the Vatican's approval.

item.title: Russia rocks Ukrainian capital with massive assault, killing at least 25
item.dc:creator: David Stern, Catherine Belton, Kostiantyn Khudov
item.description: Dozens were injured overnight in Kyiv during an attack that caused fires and explosions throughout the city.

item.title: Trump-backed truce in Lebanon, key to Iran peace deal, risks new civil war
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih, Suzan Haidamous, Mohamad El Chamaa
item.description: To reach a more durable peace agreement with Iran and open the Strait of Hormuz, Trump needs Israel to uphold the terms of a deal with Lebanon that Hezbollah says it will block by "any means necessary."
